    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP is a `}<strong parentName="p">{`JavaScript-based extensibility platform`}</strong>{` (ES6/ECMAScript 2015 compliant) that helps you automate, optimize, and extend the capabilities of your favorite Adobe Creative Cloud products. It represents a comprehensive solution that lets you `}<strong parentName="p">{`use Web technologies to build new features and tools`}</strong>{` for Desktop applications.`}</p>
    <p>{`With UXP, you can for example:`}</p>

    </ol>
    <p>{`Developing in UXP requires some basic knowledge of Web technologies, such as JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. In the following pages, we will cover the essentials you need to know to get started.`}</p>

    <p>{`It's important to note that while UXP lets you use Web technologies, it is not conceived to be a standard browser environment or replacement; not all features that a browser natively supports will be available in UXP. However, the platform provides a wide range of capabilities tailored to building powerful and creative solutions for Desktop applications.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "support-and-features"
    }}>{`Support and Features`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP is the current extensibility standard for Adobe Creative Cloud applications, supported by a constantly growing set of applications, including `}<strong parentName="p">{`Premiere`}</strong>{` (version 25.6), `}<strong parentName="p">{`Photoshop`}</strong>{`, and `}<strong parentName="p">{`InDesign`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`Plugins allow you to create a `}<strong parentName="p">{`persistent User Interface that blends seamlessly`}</strong>{` with the host application (a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`panel`}</inlineCode>{`), or an `}<strong parentName="p">{`actionable command`}</strong>{` that runs a predefined function (a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`command`}</inlineCode>{`) and can be triggered by the user via menu item—depending on how you would like the users to interact with them. You will learn all about them in the next sections.`}</p>
    <p>{`Lastly, you can `}<a parentName="p" {...{
